text: Life-changing
Formative
To broaden your horizons
An eye-opener
A catalyst
A wake-up call
To come full circle
A rite of passage
To shape
To pave the way
To set you on a path
A fresh start
If I had not gone, I would not be who I am now.
If I were not so curious, I would never have gone.
If she had studied medicine, she would not be a pilot today.
If he were not afraid of flying, he would have visited us years ago.
It is strange to think we met on that trip. If I had not taken that bus, I would never have met you, and I probably would not be doing the work I do now.
I feel the same. That month was the catalyst for everything. If I had not gone, I would still be the cautious man I was at thirty.
It really was a wake-up call for me. It broadened my horizons completely. If I were not the sort of person who says yes, I would never have got on that bus in the first place.
Funny how it works. It was such an eye-opener that it paved the way for a whole new life. And now, in a way, we have both come full circle.
We have. Same quiet lives, completely different people. If that trip had not shaped us the way it did, we would be strangers today, living smaller stories.
A whole life from one bus. If I were not a believer in second chances before, I certainly would have become one after that. It was, without question, the most formative trip of my life.
If I had not taken that trip, I would not be who I am today.
That trip was the catalyst for everything that followed.
It was a real eye-opener that broadened my horizons.
In a way, I have come full circle.

Hi Marcelo, it is Freya. You asked me about the trip that changed my life, and honestly, I could talk about it for hours. I was twenty-four, stuck in a job I did not love, and a friend invited me to travel for a month. If I had said no, I would still be in that same office right now, I am sure of it. That trip was a real wake-up call. It broadened my horizons in a way nothing else ever had. I met a woman who ran a tiny school in the mountains, and watching her changed everything for me. If I weren't the kind of person who says yes to things, I would never have gone, and I would never have found the work I do now. It is funny. I have come full circle. I live a quiet life again, but I am a completely different person. That is what one trip can do. Bye now.

Welcome back to the show. Today, why some trips change us, and most do not. Here is the strange thing. You can take ten holidays and remember none of them, and then one journey rearranges your whole life. Psychologists call these formative experiences, and they tend to share a few features. They pull you out of your routine. They put you among people who live very differently. And they arrive at a moment when you are ready, even if you do not know it. Think about it this way. If you had not been at a certain point in your life, that same trip might have meant nothing. And if you were a slightly different person, you might never have gone at all. That is the quiet mathematics of a life-changing journey. The place matters less than the timing, and the timing matters less than your willingness to say yes. So the next time a door opens, remember. The trip that changes everything rarely announces itself. It just asks whether you are brave enough to walk through.
Life-changing
Formative
To broaden your horizons
An eye-opener
A catalyst
A wake-up call
To come full circle
A rite of passage
To shape
To pave the way
To set you on a path
A fresh start
If I had not gone, I would not be here now.
If I were not so cautious, I would never have gone.
If I were not afraid, I would have visited them years ago.
That trip was the catalyst for everything that followed.
In a way, I have come full circle.
If I had not taken that trip, I would not be who I am today.
If I were not so curious, I would never have gone.
It was a real eye-opener that broadened my horizons.
